UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents the personal information and contact details of the portfolio owner, Vini Rosário.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

name.description: The full name of the portfolio owner.

aboutMeText.description: The biographical text describing Vini Rosário's story and artistic vision.

profilePictureUrl.description: URL to the stylized profile picture of Vini Rosário.

email.description: Contact email address for Vini Rosário.

linkedinUrl.description: URL to Vini Rosário's LinkedIn profile.

githubUrl.description: URL to Vini Rosário's GitHub profile.

instagramUrl.description: URL to Vini Rosário's Instagram profile.

behanceUrl.description: URL to Vini Rosário's Behance profile.

Skill.title: Skill
Skill.description: Represents a specific skill or expertise possessed by Vini Rosário.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Skill entity.

name.description: The name of the skill (e.g., 'Web Design', 'Motion Graphics').

Tool.title: Tool
Tool.description: Represents a software, technology, or equipment used by Vini Rosário.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Tool entity.

name.description: The name of the tool or technology (e.g., 'Adobe Photoshop', 'React').

iconUrl.description: URL to the icon or image representing the tool.

PortfolioVideo.title: PortfolioVideo
PortfolioVideo.description: Represents a video entry in Vini Rosário's portfolio gallery.

id.description: Unique identifier for the PortfolioVideo entity.

title.description: The title of the portfolio video.

description.description: A brief description of the video content.

videoUrl.description: The URL where the video content is hosted (e.g., YouTube, Vimeo).

thumbnailUrl.description: The URL to the thumbnail image for the video.

ContactMessage.title: ContactMessage
ContactMessage.description: Represents a message sent by a user through the portfolio's contact form.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ContactMessage entity.

senderName.description: The name of the person sending the message.

senderEmail.description: The email address of the person sending the message.

messageContent.description: The actual content of the message sent by the user.

timestamp.description: The date and time when the message was sent.

definition.description: Stores the single UserProfile document for Vini Rosário's portfolio. It's publicly readable. The 'userProfileId' will likely be a fixed ID (e.g., 'mainProfile').

params.name: userProfileId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user profile, expected to be a fixed ID like 'mainProfile'.

definition.description: A collection of Vini Rosário's skills. These documents are publicly readable and editable only by an authenticated administrator.

params.name: skillId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific skill.

definition.description: A collection of tools and technologies used by Vini Rosário. These documents are publicly readable and editable only by an authenticated administrator.

params.name: toolId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific tool.

definition.description: A collection of video entries for Vini Rosário's portfolio gallery. These documents are publicly readable and editable only by an authenticated administrator.

params.name: videoId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific portfolio video.

definition.description: A collection for messages submitted through the contact form. Any user can create a message. Only authenticated administrators can read or list these messages.

params.name: messageId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific contact message.

definition.description: A collection used for Database Access Control (DBAC) to identify administrators. The existence of a document with the user's UID here grants administrator privileges.

params.name: uid
params.description: The Firebase Authentication UID of the user who holds the admin role.

firestore.reasoning:
The proposed Firestore structure prioritizes security, scalability, and debuggability by adhering to the core design principles and mandates. 

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):** All primary collections (`userProfile`, `skills`, `tools`, `portfolioVideos`, `contactMessages`, `roles_admin`) are top-level. This design inherently eliminates the need for `get()` calls in security rules to check parent document attributes for authorization. Each document, or the collection itself, contains all necessary information for its own authorization decisions. For example, `contactMessages` documents are self-contained with sender information, and admin roles are verified by checking an entry in the dedicated `/roles_admin/{uid}` collection, not by looking up data in a parent document. This simplifies rules significantly and enables atomic operations without complex dependencies.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**

1.  **Publicly Accessible Content:** Collections like `/userProfile`, `/skills`, `/tools`, and `/portfolioVideos` are designed for public read access. All documents within these collections share the same security posture (publicly readable, admin editable). This allows for efficient `list` operations without requiring complex filtering logic in security rules. The rules simply grant `allow read, list: if true;` for public users, ensuring that `list` queries retrieve only authorized data.
2.  **User-Submitted Content (Contact Messages):** The `/contactMessages` collection allows any unauthenticated user to `create` new messages. However, `read` and `list` operations are restricted to authenticated administrators. This is achieved by security rules that permit `allow create: if true;` and `allow read, list: if get(/databases/$(database)/documents/roles_admin/$(request.auth.uid)).exists();`. This clear segregation ensures that non-admin users cannot read or list messages, satisfying QAPs by preventing unintended data exposure through `list` operations.

**Structural Segregation & Access Modeling:**
*   **Private Data (Admin-Managed):** The `/userProfile` collection is a single-document collection holding the portfolio owner's details, editable only by an authenticated admin. Other data like `/skills`, `/tools`, and `/portfolioVideos` are also read-public, write-admin, maintaining homogeneous security.
*   **Global Roles (DBAC):** An `/roles_admin/{uid}` collection is used to store administrator UIDs. The *existence* of a document at `/roles_admin/$(request.auth.uid)` grants admin privileges, adhering to the DBAC principle without relying on custom claims.
*   **Clear Ownership:** For `contactMessages`, the `senderName` and `senderEmail` are embedded within each message, providing clarity on the message origin.

This architecture provides a clean separation of concerns, simplifies security rules, and ensures efficient data access while maintaining strong security guarantees.
